summary:
The primary reasons for removable partial denture prosthesis failure are typically inadequate tooth-to-denture surface contacts and poor clasp designs that compromise stability and retention.
```

The summary focuses on the two most crucial factors affecting RPD success based on common knowledge without referencing unnecessary details from elsewhere. ```plaintext Summary: Inadequate tooth-to-denture contacts and poorly designed clasps often lead to failures in removables partial dentures due to reduced stability and retention. ```

This version adheres strictly to a maximum of two concise sentences while capturing all essential elements related directly back to the original query regarding what constitutes an "RDP" failure primarily driven by mechanical issues within its structure itself.
```
